ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific 3-in-1 white coffee with creamer and less sugar subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Coffee, instant, powder or granules — used as closest FoodEx2 anchor for instant coffee preparations
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated 3-in-1 white coffee with creamer and reduced sugar code. A0FKP (instant coffee) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for 3-in-1 instant white coffee premix. SNOMED CT does not granularly classify commercial instant coffee blends.

allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain casein)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard sachet (approximately 30 g powder dissolved in 180–200 ml hot water) represents a typical single serving of 3-in-1 instant white coffee.

nutrient_source.title: 3-in-1 White Coffee (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 3-in-1 white coffee (less sugar), cr_id D09010014. All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat not separately reported by HPB for this entry; estimated at 1.8 g/100g based on typical non-dairy creamer palm oil fat fraction (approximately 60–65% of total fat), consistent with USDA FoodData Central reference for non-dairy creamer (NDB 01069). Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, and micronutrients beyond calcium and iron are not reported by HPB for this entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant coffee with sugar and creamer
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 3-in-1 instant white coffee (less sugar) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 53 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: sweetened instant coffee with non-dairy creamer, which has a GI in the range of 50–55 due to the sugar content (sucrose GI ~65) diluted by fat and protein from the creamer. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (53 × 3.47 g carbohydrate in 30 g serving) / 100 ≈ 1.8. This is a conservative analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended. A GI of 53 is classified as 'low' according to the University of Sydney GI Database guidelines (GI <= 55 is low).

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar (6.45 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; palm-based saturated fat in non-dairy creamer may contribute to endothelial dysfunction
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this beverage because its added sugar content (6.45 g/100g) independently elevates blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention, and the saturated fat from non-dairy creamer may impair vascular function; plain unsweetened black coffee is the preferred alternative.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 6.45 g sugar/100g and rapidly absorbed liquid carbohydrates (11.55 g/100g); non-dairy creamer fat may impair insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage as it qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage under ADA guidelines — the 6.45 g/100g sugar content causes rapid postprandial glucose excursions, and the palm-based saturated fat in the non-dairy creamer may further impair insulin sensitivity; switching to plain black coffee eliminates both risks.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from palm-based non-dairy creamer (~1.8 g/100g) and added sugar (6.45 g/100g) contribute to atherogenic lipid profile and endothelial stress
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this beverage because the palm-based saturated fat in the non-dairy creamer raises LDL cholesterol and the added sugar contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia; plain unsweetened black coffee without creamer is the cardiovascular-appropriate alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Potential trans fat from hydrogenated vegetable oil in non-dairy creamer and margarine-roasted coffee; palm-based saturated fat is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage as the non-dairy creamer likely contains palm-based saturated fat and potentially hydrogenated vegetable oil (trans fat), both of which are strongly pro-inflammatory; plain unsweetened black coffee without creamer is the RA-appropriate alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Instant White Coffee (Less Sugar) is an ultra-processed instant beverage premix popular in Singapore and Malaysia, consisting of instant coffee powder, non-dairy creamer (palm oil-based), and a reduced quantity of sugar compared to the standard formulation. It is prepared by dissolving one sachet (approximately 30 g) in 180–200 ml of hot water, producing a creamy, mildly sweet coffee drink with a characteristic smooth, less bitter flavour profile compared to black coffee. The 'white coffee' style originates from Ipoh, Malaysia, where coffee beans are traditionally roasted with margarine rather than sugar, yielding a lighter roast and milder taste. The 'less sugar' variant reduces added sugar relative to the standard 3-in-1 formulation but still contains 6.45 g of sugar per 100 g of powder, classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage under ADA and WHO guidelines. Per 100 g of powder, it provides 74 kcal, 0.45 g protein, 2.89 g fat (predominantly saturated from palm-based creamer), and 11.55 g carbohydrate with 6.45 g sugar and 0.52 g dietary fibre. Sodium is low at 31 mg/100g. Clinically, this beverage is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its sugar content and liquid carbohydrate absorption kinetics, and it poses moderate concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat from the non-dairy creamer. The non-dairy creamer may also contain hydrogenated vegetable oil, introducing potential trans fat, which is a concern for rheumatoid arthritis and cardiovascular health. It is commonly consumed at breakfast and is widely available in Singapore and Malaysian convenience stores, supermarkets, and coffee shops.
common_user_queries: Is 3-in-1 white coffee less sugar healthy?
How many calories in 3-in-1 white coffee less sugar?
Is instant white coffee suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of 3-in-1 white coffee less sugar?
Is 3-in-1 white coffee less sugar better than regular white coffee?
What is white coffee made of?
How much fat is in instant white coffee premix?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09010014), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central non-dairy creamer reference as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. GI is an analogous estimate as no direct measurement exists. Overall confidence is high for macronutrients and medium for GI.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.8 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central non-dairy creamer reference (NDB 01069), as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. This is an approximation based on the typical palm oil fat fraction (60–65% of total fat) in non-dairy creamers. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this commercial instant white coffee premix are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 by HPB (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database entry D09010014). 4. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened instant coffee with non-dairy creamer entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; direct GI measurement for this specific product is not available. A GI of 53 is classified as 'low' according to the University of Sydney GI Database guidelines (GI <= 55 is low). 5. The 'less sugar' designation reduces sugar relative to the standard formulation but does not eliminate it; the product still qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage at 6.45 g sugar/100g. 6. Dietary tags 'low_sodium' and 'low_calorie' from the input are retained as contextual tags only; clinically, this product is not recommended for diabetes or hypertension management despite these tags. 7. Trans fat content is not confirmed by HPB; the input health suitability notes suggest potential trans fat from margarine-roasted coffee and hydrogenated creamer oil — this remains unconfirmed and is set to -1 pending label verification.
